2603.19711.title: EvoTaxo: Building and Evolving Taxonomy from Social Media Streams
2603.19711.paper_summary: Constructing taxonomies from social media corpora is challenging because posts are short, noisy, semantically entangled, and temporally dynamic. Existing taxonomy induction methods are largely designed for static corpora and often struggle to balance robustness, scalability, and sensitivity to evolving discourse. We propose EvoTaxo, a LLM-based framework for building and evolving taxonomies from temporally ordered social media streams. Rather than clustering raw posts directly, EvoTaxo converts each post into a structured draft action over the current taxonomy, accumulates structural evidence over time windows, and consolidates candidate edits through dual-view clustering that combines semantic similarity with temporal locality. A refinement-and-arbitration procedure then selects reliable edits before execution, while each node maintains a concept memory bank to preserve semantic boundaries over time. Experiments on two Reddit corpora show that EvoTaxo produces more balanced taxonomies than baselines, with clearer post-to-leaf assignment, better corpus coverage at comparable taxonomy size, and stronger structural quality. A case study on the Reddit community /r/ICE_Raids further shows that EvoTaxo captures meaningful temporal shifts in discourse. Our codebase is available here.
2603.19711.paper_summary_zh: 從社交媒體語料庫構建分類法是具有挑戰性的，因為帖子短小、雜訊多、語義交織且隨時間變化。現有的分類法引導方法主要是為靜態語料庫設計的，通常難以平衡穩健性、可擴展性和對不斷演變的話語的敏感性。我們提出了EvoTaxo，一個基於LLM的框架，用於從時間排序的社交媒體流中構建和演變分類法。EvoTaxo不是直接對原始帖子進行聚類，而是將每個帖子轉換為對當前分類法的結構化草擬行動，隨著時間窗口累積結構證據，並通過結合語義相似性和時間局部性的雙視角聚類來整合候選編輯。然後，精煉和仲裁程序在執行之前選擇可靠的編輯，而每個節點維護一個概念記憶庫，以隨時間保留語義邊界。在兩個Reddit語料庫上的實驗顯示，EvoTaxo產生的分類法比基準更為平衡，具有更清晰的帖子到葉子的分配，在可比較的分類法大小下擁有更好的語料庫覆蓋率，以及更強的結構質量。對Reddit社區/r/ICE_Raids的案例研究進一步顯示，EvoTaxo捕捉到了話語中有意義的時間變化。我們的代碼庫可在此處獲得。
2603.19711.author: Yiyang Li et.al.
2603.19711.authors: Yiyang Li, Tianyi Ma, Yanfang Ye
